Shrine | image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki|Q335423}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{jalink|Takatsuki|Q335423}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{jalink|Takayama Ukon|Q749873}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Haiden | location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Takatsuki|Q335423}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{jalink|Takatsuki|Q335423}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Uda|Q349267}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{jalink|Takayama Ukon|Q749873}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{jalink|Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine Shrine | image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Shrine | image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QNomi Shrine (Q11646130)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)}}[[Category:qq]] {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{DEFAULTSORT:のみしんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. == * {'text': '大阪府高槻市野見町6-6', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府高槻市天神町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646130}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Nomi Shrine | image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:qq]]

== * {'text': '大阪府高槻市野見町6-6', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪府高槻市天神町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:野見神社 (高槻市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11646130}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | name = Nomi Shrine | image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:qq]]

[[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:高槻市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:摂津国の式内社]] [[Category:qq]]

It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]

Haiden | location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}} Nomi-cho 6-6 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} | founded = Late 9th century | status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | annual_festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomi Shrine''' (のみじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine in Nomi-cho, {{ill|lt=Takatsuki|Q335423|WD=Q335423|1=Takatsuki}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is treated as a candidate for the Engishiki Nomi Shrine and was formerly ranked as a gosha. === History === In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}} (late 9th century), an epidemic struck the area; after an oracle, the deity Gozu Tenno (identified with Susanoo) was enshrined and the epidemic subsided, leading to construction of a shrine. When Takatsuki Castle was built, the shrine was revered as a castle guardian. In the 16th century, while {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|WD=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} ruled Takatsuki, the shrine buildings inside the castle were destroyed and lands confiscated. In 1619, lord {{ill|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu|Q5368670|WD=Q5368670|1=Matsudaira Ienobu}} rebuilt the shrine buildings. After the 1868 separation of Shinto and Buddhism, the principal deity name was changed to Susanoo-no-Mikoto; Nomi no Sukune was additionally enshrined and the shrine renamed "Nomi Shrine." Because of this late renaming, some scholars argue the actual descendant of the Engishiki Nomi Shrine is the Nomi Shrine subsidiary of Kamigu Tenmangu in Takatsuki. It was briefly ranked as prefectural shrine in 1871, then demoted to gosha in 1872 for the above reasons. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Nomi no Sukune|Q965742|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomi-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Nomi no Sukune}} === Precinct === === Main buildings === * Honden - rebuilt in 1682 * Haiden - rebuilt in 2003 * Noh stage * Shrine office === Subsidiary and auxiliary shrines === * Nagai Shrine (founded 1793; associated with the Nagai lords of Takatsuki) * Kojima Shrine * Shisha Myojin (consolidation of four shrines in Taisho period) * Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine (branch from Nishinomiya Shrine, founded 1956) * Gokoku Shrine (for Takatsuki war dead) Nomijinja02.jpg|Karamon of Nagai Shrine Tessen.png|Crest used at Nagai Shrine === Annual events === * New Year's festival (Jan 1) * Tondo fire rite (Jan 15) * Setsubun festival (Feb 3) * Baby crying sumo dedication (Apr 29) * Summer purification (Jun 30) * Autumn grand festival (second Monday of October) * Niiname-sai (Nov 23) * Year-end purification (Dec 31) === Cultural properties === === Takatsuki City designated tangible cultural properties === * Nagai Shrine buildings (honden and haiden), with 4 munafuda and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema (designated June 14, 2005) * Nagai Shrine karamon (designated June 14, 2005) * 17 historical materials relating to Nagai Naokiyo (deposited at Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum) === Nearby === * Catholic Takatsuki Church * Japan Claretian Missionaries headquarters monastery * Maria Immaculata Kindergarten * Takatsuki Castle Park Arts and Culture Theater * Takatsuki Castle Site History Museum * Ruins of Takatsuki Castle and nearby historical park areas === Notes === {{reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomi Shrine] (official site) * Takatsuki city online history pages on Nomi Shrine and Nagai Shrine Karamon {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nomi Shrine (Q11646130)]][[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:高槻市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]]
